results.label: Suspected pulmonary embolism
results.context:
General medicine
Adult
validated clinical intent workup
intent: Suspected pulmonary embolism
intent_id: suspected_pe_v1
intent tags: dyspnea; hypoxia; chest_pain; cardiopulmonary; perfusion; shock; vitals; vital_signs; lower_extremity; vascular_exam; dvt; vte; edema; pulmonary_embolism; d_dimer; ctpa; vq_scan; clinical_probability; anticoagulation_safety
clinical bundles: suspected_pe; chest_pain
required domains: vitals; work of breathing/lungs; cardiac strain/perfusion; DVT leg exam
avoid labels: PMI; Abdominal palpation; Bowel sounds; CVA tenderness; Vibration sense

safety.label: Blood pressure
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: hypotension, hypertension, shock physiology, pain response, medication effect, or disposition-changing instability.
safety.management_change: Abnormal blood pressure can change acuity, fluids, antihypertensive decisions, endocrine-crisis treatment, monitoring, and escalation.
safety.evidence: AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; JAMA_RCE

safety.label: Heart rate
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: tachycardia, bradycardia, irregular rhythm, shock physiology, adrenergic state, infection, pain, or medication effect.
safety.management_change: Abnormal heart rate can change ECG/telemetry urgency, volume or endocrine-crisis assessment, infection severity, medication review, and escalation.
safety.evidence: AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; JAMA_RCE

safety.label: Respiratory rate
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: respiratory distress, compensation, shock physiology, acid-base stress, sedation, or cardiopulmonary deterioration.
safety.management_change: Abnormal respiratory rate or labored breathing can change oxygen/ventilation assessment, acid-base urgency, monitoring level, and escalation.
safety.evidence: AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; JAMA_RCE

safety.label: Oxygen saturation / support
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: hypoxemia, escalating oxygen need, respiratory failure risk, shock physiology, pulmonary disease, or cardiopulmonary deterioration.
safety.management_change: Abnormal oxygen saturation or rising oxygen requirement can change respiratory support, imaging urgency, pulmonary/infectious/cardiac framing, monitoring level, and escalation.
safety.evidence: AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; JAMA_RCE

safety.label: Temperature
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: fever, hypothermia, infection, drug reaction, endocrine crisis, inflammatory disease, or systemic illness.
safety.management_change: Fever or hypothermia can change infection/endocrine-crisis workup, isolation, medication decisions, monitoring, and escalation.
safety.evidence: AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; JAMA_RCE

history.label: Any sudden shortness of breath, pleuritic chest pain, hemoptysis, syncope, prior VTE, recent surgery/immobility/travel, estrogen or pregnancy/postpartum state, cancer, unilateral leg swelling or pain, or bleeding risk?
history.reason: PE/DVT probability and treatment safety: cardiopulmonary symptoms, VTE risk factors, prior clot, DVT symptoms, and anticoagulation or thrombolysis contraindications.
history.management_change: These findings change PERC/Wells/Geneva framing, D-dimer versus imaging strategy, compression ultrasound threshold, empiric anticoagulation discussion, bleeding-risk documentation, and ED/monitoring escalation.

core.label: Lateral lung sounds
core.reason: Checks work of breathing and alternate pulmonary diagnoses while PE remains on the table.
core.diagnostic_target: Respiratory distress, wheeze, crackles, focal diminished sounds, or other findings that point to PE severity or competing lung diagnoses.
core.management_change: Work of breathing or focal lung findings can change oxygen strategy, imaging review, treatment for alternatives such as pneumonia/pneumothorax, and escalation.

core.label: JVP
core.reason: Assesses rhythm, murmur, heart failure/strain, and perfusion clues.
core.diagnostic_target: elevated right-sided filling pressure or venous congestion
core.management_change: Abnormal congestion findings can support diuresis decisions, escalation of respiratory support assessment, daily volume trending, or need for cardiac evaluation in context.
core.evidence: Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension

core.label: Heart sounds
core.reason: Assesses rhythm, murmur, heart failure/strain, and perfusion clues.
core.diagnostic_target: focal consolidation, effusion, wheeze, work of breathing, or ventilation abnormality
core.management_change: New focal pulmonary findings can prompt oxygen/support reassessment, imaging review, antibiotics/bronchodilator consideration, or escalation.
core.evidence: JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds

core.label: Posterior tibial pulses
core.reason: Checks unilateral swelling or vascular findings relevant to VTE probability and anticoagulation framing.
core.diagnostic_target: DVT clue: asymmetric leg swelling/tenderness/perfusion abnormality or limb-risk finding.
core.management_change: DVT-compatible findings can strengthen VTE probability, ultrasound urgency, anticoagulation framing, and documentation of limb/perfusion risk.
core.evidence: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources

core.label: Dorsalis pedis pulses
core.reason: Checks unilateral swelling or vascular findings relevant to VTE probability and anticoagulation framing.
core.diagnostic_target: DVT clue: asymmetric leg swelling/tenderness/perfusion abnormality or limb-risk finding.
core.management_change: DVT-compatible findings can strengthen VTE probability, ultrasound urgency, anticoagulation framing, and documentation of limb/perfusion risk.
core.evidence: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources

core.label: Radial pulses
core.reason: Assesses rhythm, murmur, heart failure/strain, and perfusion clues.
core.diagnostic_target: perfusion deficit, vascular disease, or limb ischemia clue
core.management_change: Abnormal vitals or perfusion findings can trigger urgent reassessment, resuscitation, sepsis/shock pathway review, or escalation.
core.evidence: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources

core.label: Femoral pulses
core.reason: Checks unilateral swelling or vascular findings relevant to VTE probability and anticoagulation framing.
core.diagnostic_target: DVT clue: asymmetric leg swelling/tenderness/perfusion abnormality or limb-risk finding.
core.management_change: DVT-compatible findings can strengthen VTE probability, ultrasound urgency, anticoagulation framing, and documentation of limb/perfusion risk.
core.evidence: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources

core.label: Posterior thorax inspection
core.reason: Checks work of breathing and alternate pulmonary diagnoses while PE remains on the table.
core.diagnostic_target: Respiratory distress, wheeze, crackles, focal diminished sounds, or other findings that point to PE severity or competing lung diagnoses.
core.management_change: Work of breathing or focal lung findings can change oxygen strategy, imaging review, treatment for alternatives such as pneumonia/pneumothorax, and escalation.

tests.label: PE probability, D-dimer, and imaging pathway
tests.reason: Suspected PE testing should be anchored to clinical probability so low-risk patients avoid unnecessary imaging while high-risk or unstable patients are escalated promptly.
tests.diagnostic_target: Diagnostic pathway selection: low/intermediate probability with D-dimer rule-out potential versus high probability or unstable PE requiring imaging/escalation.
tests.management_change: A negative appropriate D-dimer can avoid imaging in low/intermediate probability; high probability, positive D-dimer, hypoxemia, syncope, or instability changes CTPA/V/Q urgency, anticoagulation planning, and disposition.

tests.label: DVT and right-heart strain evaluation
tests.reason: The bedside workup should connect leg findings and cardiopulmonary severity to DVT confirmation and PE risk stratification.
tests.diagnostic_target: VTE confirmation and PE severity: proximal DVT evidence, right ventricular strain, myocardial injury, hemodynamic compromise, and oxygenation trend.
tests.management_change: A proximal DVT can support treatment decisions when PE imaging is not feasible; RV strain, positive biomarkers, hypotension, or worsening oxygen need changes monitoring, PE response-team/thrombolysis discussion, and disposition.

red_flags.label: High-risk PE escalation cues
red_flags.reason: Suspected PE must screen for high-risk physiology because unstable PE changes the pathway from routine confirmation to urgent escalation.
red_flags.diagnostic_target: High-risk PE physiology: shock, hypotension, syncope, severe hypoxemia, altered mentation, persistent tachycardia/tachypnea, RV strain, or arrest.
red_flags.management_change: These findings change disposition, monitoring level, urgent imaging/bedside echo strategy, anticoagulation urgency, PE response-team activation, and thrombolysis/embolectomy discussion.

red_flags.label: Anticoagulation and thrombolysis safety cues
red_flags.reason: A PE workup must document treatment-safety constraints because the diagnostic and management pathway often hinges on anticoagulation and sometimes thrombolysis.
red_flags.diagnostic_target: Treatment-risk context: bleeding risk, recent surgery/trauma, intracranial risk, severe hypertension, pregnancy/postpartum, renal/contrast limitation, fall risk, or pending procedure.
red_flags.management_change: Safety cues change anticoagulation choice/dose/timing, imaging selection, consultation, thrombolysis eligibility, reversal planning, monitoring level, and shared risk documentation.

evidence_metadata.diagnostic_target: PE/DVT probability and treatment safety: cardiopulmonary symptoms, VTE risk factors, prior clot, DVT symptoms, and anticoagulation or thrombolysis contraindications.

evidence_metadata.label: PE probability, D-dimer, and imaging pathway
evidence_metadata.reason: Diagnostic pathway selection: low/intermediate probability with D-dimer rule-out potential versus high probability or unstable PE requiring imaging/escalation.
evidence_metadata.diagnostic_target: Diagnostic pathway selection: low/intermediate probability with D-dimer rule-out potential versus high probability or unstable PE requiring imaging/escalation.

evidence_metadata.label: DVT and right-heart strain evaluation
evidence_metadata.reason: VTE confirmation and PE severity: proximal DVT evidence, right ventricular strain, myocardial injury, hemodynamic compromise, and oxygenation trend.
evidence_metadata.diagnostic_target: VTE confirmation and PE severity: proximal DVT evidence, right ventricular strain, myocardial injury, hemodynamic compromise, and oxygenation trend.

evidence_metadata.label: High-risk PE escalation cues
evidence_metadata.reason: High-risk PE physiology: shock, hypotension, syncope, severe hypoxemia, altered mentation, persistent tachycardia/tachypnea, RV strain, or arrest.
evidence_metadata.diagnostic_target: High-risk PE physiology: shock, hypotension, syncope, severe hypoxemia, altered mentation, persistent tachycardia/tachypnea, RV strain, or arrest.

evidence_metadata.label: Anticoagulation and thrombolysis safety cues
evidence_metadata.reason: Treatment-risk context: bleeding risk, recent surgery/trauma, intracranial risk, severe hypertension, pregnancy/postpartum, renal/contrast limitation, fall risk, or pending procedure.
evidence_metadata.diagnostic_target: Treatment-risk context: bleeding risk, recent surgery/trauma, intracranial risk, severe hypertension, pregnancy/postpartum, renal/contrast limitation, fall risk, or pending procedure.

evidence_metadata.label: Blood pressure
evidence_metadata.reason: Basic bedside safety: hypotension, hypertension, shock physiology, pain response, medication effect, or disposition-changing instability.
evidence_metadata.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: hypotension, hypertension, shock physiology, pain response, medication effect, or disposition-changing instability.
evidence_metadata.evidence: AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; JAMA_RCE

evidence_metadata.label: Heart rate
evidence_metadata.reason: Basic bedside safety: tachycardia, bradycardia, irregular rhythm, shock physiology, adrenergic state, infection, pain, or medication effect.

top_retrieved.label: Respiratory rate

top_retrieved.evidence: Surviving Sepsis Campaign guideline; vital-sign trend interpreted in clinical context
